appendMonsterAbilities.name: Enweb
appendMonsterAbilities.rulesText: The target cannot move and has disadvantage on all skill checks until it scores 10 on a STR check.

appendMonsterAbilities.name: Constrict

check.notes: Only usable against an enemy within reach that the serpent has advantage against.

appendMonsterAbilities.rulesText: The target takes 3 physical damage and cannot move on its next turn.

appendMonsterAbilities.name: Charge
appendMonsterAbilities.rulesText: After it moves, the boar deals +1 physical damage on its next attack this turn.

appendMonsterAbilities.name: Shadow Theft
appendMonsterAbilities.rulesText: The nocturi steals the target's shadow. The target takes +2 magic damage whenever it takes damage. The target can reclaim its shadow by damaging the nocturi.

appendMonsterAbilities.name: Celestial Song
appendMonsterAbilities.rulesText: Once per turn, the angel can restore 3 HP to a creature it can see.

appendMonsterAbilities.name: Kinetic Barrage
appendMonsterAbilities.rulesText: The target takes 1 physical damage and the deva can repeat this attack.

appendMonsterAbilities.name: Mana Sap
appendMonsterAbilities.rulesText: The target loses 3 MP. If the target has no more MP, it loses HP instead. If the voidleech kills a humanoid with mana sap, it can transform into a voidwalker after an hour-long metamorphosis (the voidleech is defenseless during this transformation).

appendMonsterAbilities.name: Dragonfire
appendMonsterAbilities.rulesText: When the dragon deals magic damage, the target regains an equal amount of MP. The target takes half of the damage again at the end of their next turn.

appendMonsterAbilities.name: Regrowth
appendMonsterAbilities.rulesText: When the hydra takes 3 or more physical damage at once, its head splits into two new heads (maximum eight heads). The hydra gains a +1 bonus to CHA, INS, and PRW for each new head that it grows. The CHA bonus increases the hydra's maximum HP.

appendMonsterAbilities.name: Petrifying Gaze

check.notes: Target must be able to see the basilisk.

appendMonsterAbilities.rulesText: The target begins turning to stone, and loses one movement. If they fail a skill check on their next turn, they lose a skill check on every subsequent turn. If they fail another skill check after that, they are fully petrified, and cannot act.

appendMonsterAbilities.name: Devour

check.notes: The basilisk picks up a petrified enemy within its reach (its saliva reverses the petrification).

appendMonsterAbilities.rulesText: The basilisk swallows the target. The target takes 5 magic damage at the start of each of its subsequent turns, but can ignore the basilisk's tough trait.

appendMonsters.name: Roc
appendMonsters.loreText: Colossal birds of prey, rocs can shroud an entire village in shadow with their outstretched wings. They normally hunt large herbivores, and will gladly eat any mortal that approaches its nest.

appendMonsters.name: Giant Spider
appendMonsters.loreText: Giant spiders evolved to hunt greater prey than their smaller cousins. Their webs are like steel and their venom turns the blood ice cold.

appendMonsters.name: Bear
appendMonsters.loreText: Fearsome woodland beasts, bears represent strength in many cultures.

appendMonsters.name: Tiger
appendMonsters.loreText: Graceful jungle cats, tigers represent ferocity in many cultures.

appendMonsters.name: Shark
appendMonsters.loreText: Apex predators of the oceans, sharks have a keen sense for blood.

appendMonsters.name: Serpent
appendMonsters.loreText: Cold-blooded ambush predators, serpents represent cunning in many cultures.

appendMonsters.name: Boar
appendMonsters.loreText: Temperamental hogs of the woodlands, wild boar defend themselves with bristled hides and curved tusks. Many carnivores hunt wild boar, but they do not go down without a fight.

appendMonsters.name: Horse
appendMonsters.loreText: Valiant steeds to people across the pillar worlds, horses represent freedom in many cultures.

appendMonsters.name: Wolf
appendMonsters.loreText: Woodland hunters, wolves symbolize fear in many cultures. Their haunting howl sends shivers down the spines of vulnerable travelers, and their pack tactics allow them to take down prey many times their size.

appendMonsters.name: Elk
appendMonsters.loreText: Regal beasts of the woodlands and prairies, elk represent nobility in many cultures. Elk are preyed on by many of the carnivorous creatures of the pillar worlds, relying on their speed and keen hearing to survive.

appendMonsters.name: Divine Avatar
appendMonsters.loreText: The gods project their power into the material world through divine avatars. Though gods are immortal, their divine power can be claimed if their avatar is slain.

appendMonsters.name: Nocturi
appendMonsters.loreText: Immortal embodiments of the night sky, nocturi wield incredible powers of darkness. Shadowy figures dappled with cold starlight, the nocturi float silently through the aether. They collect the shadows of mortals, draining their spirits.

appendMonsters.name: Seraph
appendMonsters.loreText: Crusaders of justice created by the gods of honour, seraphs hunt down and purge sources of corruption.

appendMonsters.name: Angel

equipment.name: Diamond Harp
equipment.rulesText: The angel gains advantage on its next skill check upon successful defense.

appendMonsters.loreText: Servants of the gods of love, angels lend their divine protection to mortals in their hour of greatest need.

appendMonsters.name: Wingbreaker
appendMonsters.loreText: Angels who have fallen from grace, wingbreakers are dangerous monsters. Their celestial powers bring a dissonant cacophony, disrupting the balance of everything they touch. Wingbreakers earned their name by hunting down and killing angels to absorb their divine essence.
notes: Trait line in source ends with a trailing comma; interpreted as the 5 traits listed.

appendMonsters.name: Pegasus
appendMonsters.loreText: Winged horses, pegasi serve as steeds to the divines.

appendMonsters.name: Deva
appendMonsters.loreText: Divine artisans created by the gods of craft, deva are six-armed immortals who forge the kingdoms of the heavens.

appendMonsters.name: Voidwalker
appendMonsters.loreText: Dreadful beings of crackling shadow, voidwalkers feed on spirits and corrupt their energy. Voidwalkers are conduits for the black magic of the void, which they lend to vile monsters and wicked mortals. Voidwalkers spread ruin and despair throughout the pillar worlds, darkening the dreams of their inhabitants so that more of their kind may crawl out from the abyss. Voidwalkers are extremely sensitive to light, and usually create hives in dark places like caves and dungeons.

appendMonsters.name: Voidleech
appendMonsters.loreText: Slithering little monsters that enter reality through the nightmares of mortals, voidleeches lurk in the dark. Voidleeches latch onto mortals and drain their spirit until nothing but a hollow husk remains. Once a voidleech has absorbed a mortal spirit, it metamorphosizes into a voidwalker.

appendMonsters.name: Ancient Dragon
appendMonsters.loreText: After slumbering for a thousand years, dragons amass enough raw mana to frighten the gods. Their rumbling call is the thunder that shakes the mountains and their fiery maw burns with the setting sun. Ancient dragons are few and far between, but reverberations of their immense power can be felt throughout the land. Their blood soaked legacy built the pillar worlds, and now they reign as undisputed sovereigns.

appendMonsters.name: Young Dragon
appendMonsters.loreText: Dragons do not fully mature until they are a hundred years old, but even a young dragon can destroy entire cities. During this formative age, dragons choose a den, mate with other young dragons, and develop opinions of mortals that often last thousands of years. Dragons are much more active during their youth, and often settle into an indefinite slumber once they fully mature.

appendMonsters.name: Hydra
appendMonsters.loreText: Aquatic dragonoids with long snaking necks, hydra are famous for their regenerative abilities. The collective cry of a hydra's many heads can be heard far and wide, freezing their prey in fear.

appendMonsters.name: Basilisk
appendMonsters.loreText: Magical serpents that turn their prey to stone with their petrifying gaze, basilisks are descended from a bloodline of dragons that was imprisoned underground for millennia. Though basilisks don't have wings or breathe fire, they share their nearly indestructible scales and vengeful wrath with true dragons. The only way to cure a basilisk's petrification is by exposing the victim to the basilisk's saliva.

appendMonsters.name: Dragon Hatchling
appendMonsters.loreText: Though each dragon bloodline varies, some of them are as small as cats when they first emerge from their eggs. Their veins still flow with raw mana, but it takes years for their bodies to fully develop and for them to refine their hunting skills. If socialized during their infancy, some bloodlines can grow up to live in harmony with mortals.

appendMonsters.name: Drakken
appendMonsters.loreText: Large reptilian monsters, drakkens have similar bodies to dragons but do not have wings or magical abilities. Nevertheless, they are vicious predators built for speed and survival. They have tough scales and sharp claws, running down large prey and eviscerating them.

appendMonsters.name: Wyvern
appendMonsters.loreText: Often confused for true dragons, wyverns can be identified by their lack of front legs. Wyverns are also significantly smaller and less intelligent than dragons when fully grown. Some mortals raise wyverns from birth, training them as flying mounts.

appendMonsters.name: Drakeling
appendMonsters.loreText: Tiny cousins of true dragons, drakelings gather in swarms to take down larger prey. Only growing to the size of a hawk, drakelings are sometimes kept as pets or trained to hunt vermin. Though they lack the magical properties of true dragons, drakelings have sharp teeth and can be dangerous to travellers.
